title: Discovery Meeting - Mi Casa al Dia Property Information System

participants.name: Mi Casa al Dia Representative
participants.role: Operations Lead / Architect at Mi Casa al Dia

participants.name: Daniel Cardona
participants.role: Mentioned at start, unclear if joined

summary: Discovery meeting with Mi Casa al Dia, a digital real estate company that sells Colombian properties to immigrants in the US. Their core pain point is the extremely manual and time-consuming process of consolidating property information from 25+ construction companies (each with different Excel/PDF formats) to respond to client inquiries. A phased solution was proposed: (1) data consolidation into a PostgreSQL database, (2) an agile update mechanism with drag-and-drop file ingestion, and (3) WhatsApp integration for automated client responses. Next step is signing a confidentiality agreement, sharing sample data, and building a proof of concept by the following week.

discussion_points.description: Mi Casa al Dia manages 250+ real estate projects from 25+ construction companies. Each company sends data in different formats (Excel, PDF). Responding to a single client inquiry requires searching across the website, multiple Excel files, PDF brochures, Google Drive, WhatsApp groups, and email. A simple city-based query can take 5+ minutes and involves consulting 4-5 different sources.

Three-phase approach agreed upon: Phase 1 - Initial data consolidation into a PostgreSQL database with field mapping rules. Phase 2 - Agile update mechanism via a web app with drag-and-drop file upload that auto-detects fields from varying Excel/PDF formats. Phase 3 - WhatsApp integration for automated message generation and client response.

Key fields identified for the consolidated database: constructor name, project name, active status, last update date, published status, department, municipality, sector/zone, typology (apartment/house/lot), phase (launch/construction/immediate delivery), area range, number of units, unit types, VIS classification, price range, parking, bedrooms, bathrooms, and project images.

Clients arrive via WhatsApp integrated with HubSpot, but HubSpot's 24-hour Meta messaging window is restrictive. The team transfers clients to corporate WhatsApp Business lines for continued communication. Responses are manually drafted with project details, links to the website, brochure information, and payment plans.

Mi Casa al Dia has a custom-coded website (not Wix or similar platform) with an admin panel. Updating prices requires manually editing each project and each unit type one by one. Price updates from a single large constructor with 80 projects is extremely time-consuming. Data must be updated in both the admin panel and the internal Excel macro separately.

Mi Casa al Dia is part of a large corporate group (1000+ employees) serving immigrants in the US with legal, tax, and insurance services. The parent company enforces strict technology policies and mandates HubSpot as the CRM. The IT department prioritizes immigration operations, leaving smaller subsidiaries like Mi Casa al Dia without tech support but bound by restrictions.

Mi Casa al Dia currently operates with only 3 people. They want to scale to handle more clients but lack the infrastructure for rapid, informed responses. The immigration political climate in the US has also impacted their target market, pushing them to explore new markets.

Discussed the possibility of connecting the new consolidated database to the existing website so that uploading data once automatically updates the public-facing site. This would eliminate the current dual-update process. Feasibility depends on the website's technical architecture, which the client will share.

action_items.description: Send or agree on a confidentiality/NDA agreement before sharing proprietary data
action_items.owner: Both parties (Mi Casa al Dia to send theirs, or Daniel's team to provide one)

action_items.description: Share sample Excel files from various construction companies (different formats)
Share sample PDF brochures from construction companies
Share the website technical specifications and architecture details
Define the complete field mapping rules (how different constructor formats map to unified schema)
Build proof of concept for data consolidation and recurring update mechanism
action_items.deadline: By Monday or Tuesday of the following week

action_items.description: Review NDA options with Cardona and send to Mi Casa al Dia
Schedule follow-up meeting to review proof of concept

decisions: Adopt a phased approach: Phase 1 (Data Consolidation), Phase 2 (Update Mechanism), Phase 3 (WhatsApp Integration)
Start with a proof of concept focused on data consolidation and agile updating before committing to the full solution
Sign a confidentiality agreement before sharing any proprietary constructor data
Use WhatsApp for ongoing communication between meetings
Evaluate the existing website architecture to determine if the new database can be connected to it or if a new frontend is needed
next_steps: Finalize and sign confidentiality agreement (either from Mi Casa al Dia or from Daniel's team)
Mi Casa al Dia shares sample Excel files, PDFs, and website technical specs
Daniel builds a proof of concept for data consolidation with sample data
Follow-up meeting scheduled for Monday or Tuesday of the following week to review the proof of concept
Based on proof of concept results, define scope and next phases of the project

diagrams.title: Current Manual Information Lookup Workflow
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    A[Client Inquiry via WhatsApp or HubSpot] --> B{What type of query?}
    B -->|By City| C[Search Website by City]
    B -->|By Project| D[Search Website by Project]
    C --> E[Open Each Project One by One]
    D --> E
    E --> F[Check Internal Excel Macro]
    F --> G[Search Google Drive for Constructor Folder]
    G --> H{File Type?}
    H -->|Excel| I[Open Constructor Excel]
    H -->|PDF| J[Open PDF Brochure]
    I --> K[Extract Relevant Data Manually]
    J --> K
    K --> L[Draft WhatsApp Message]
    L --> M[Send Response to Client]
    M --> N{Client Wants More Detail?}
    N -->|Yes| O[Deep Dive into Specific Project]
    N -->|No| P[End Conversation]
    O --> G

diagrams.title: Proposed System Architecture
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart LR
    subgraph DataSources[Data Sources]
        EX[Excel Files from Constructors]
        PD[PDF Brochures]
        WS[Existing Website Data]
    end
    subgraph Ingestion[Ingestion Layer]
        UP[Drag and Drop Upload]
        FD[Format Detection Engine]
        FM[Field Mapping and Validation]
        RV[Admin Review and Approve]
    end
    subgraph Storage[Central Database]
        PG[(PostgreSQL Database)]
    end
    subgraph Frontend[User Interfaces]
        AD[Admin Web App]
        SR[Search Interface]
        PW[Public Website]
    end
    subgraph Integration[Communication]
        WA[WhatsApp Business API]
        MG[Message Generator]
    end
    EX --> UP
    PD --> UP
    WS --> FD
    UP --> FD
    FD --> FM
    FM --> RV
    RV --> PG
    PG --> AD
    PG --> SR
    PG --> PW
    SR --> MG
    MG --> WA

diagrams.title: Phased Implementation Roadmap
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    subgraph Phase1[Phase 1 - Data Consolidation]
        P1A[Collect Sample Excel and PDF Files]
        P1B[Define Unified Data Schema]
        P1C[Build Field Mapping Rules]
        P1D[Import Data into PostgreSQL]
        P1E[Proof of Concept Demo]
        P1A --> P1B --> P1C --> P1D --> P1E
    end
    subgraph Phase2[Phase 2 - Agile Update Mechanism]
        P2A[Web App with Drag and Drop Upload]
        P2B[Auto Format Detection for Excel and PDF]
        P2C[Admin Review and Approval UI]
        P2D[Auto Sync with Public Website]
        P2A --> P2B --> P2C --> P2D
    end
    subgraph Phase3[Phase 3 - WhatsApp Integration]
        P3A[Search Interface by City and Project]
        P3B[Auto Message Template Generator]
        P3C[WhatsApp API Integration]
        P3D[Automated Client Responses]
        P3A --> P3B --> P3C --> P3D
    end
    Phase1 --> Phase2 --> Phase3

diagrams.title: Client Inquiry Response Sequence
diagrams.mermaid_code:
sequenceDiagram
    participant CL as Client
    participant HS as HubSpot WhatsApp
    participant AG as Sales Agent
    participant WB as WhatsApp Business
    participant DB as Project Database
    participant CT as Constructor
    CL->>HS: Initial inquiry about a city or project
    HS->>AG: Notification of new lead
    AG->>HS: Initial greeting within 24h window
    AG->>WB: Transfer client to corporate WhatsApp
    AG->>DB: Search projects by city or criteria
    DB-->>AG: Matching projects with basic details
    AG->>WB: Send formatted options to client
    WB->>CL: Project options with prices and links
    CL->>WB: Interest in specific project
    WB->>AG: Client selected a project
    AG->>DB: Get detailed project info
    DB-->>AG: Full details and brochure and payment plans
    AG->>WB: Send detailed info to client
    WB->>CL: Detailed project info with images
    CL->>WB: Wants to proceed
    AG->>CT: Request availability and quotation
    CT-->>AG: Availability and formal quote
    AG->>WB: Forward quotation to client
    WB->>CL: Final quotation and next steps
